message:
fix(auth): implement local OAuth callback server for browser redirect capture

The OAuth login flow (e.g. `/login openai-codex`) sets redirect_uri to
http://localhost:1455/auth/callback but previously no server was listening
on that port, causing "connection refused" in the browser after the user
completes authorization.

Changes:
- Add `redirect_uri` field to `OAuthStartInfo` so callers know which URI
  was used in the authorization request.
- Implement `OAuthCallbackServer`: a background `TcpListener` that binds
  to the port from the redirect URI, accepts exactly one connection,
  extracts the authorization code from the HTTP GET request line, sends a
  friendly "Authorization successful" HTML page to the browser, and
  delivers the callback URL through an mpsc channel.
- Add `start_oauth_callback_server()` and `redirect_uri_needs_callback_server()`
  public helpers, plus `parse_port_from_uri()` for extracting the port.
- Wire the callback server into both code paths:
  - CLI (`main.rs`): races the callback server against manual stdin paste,
    polling both non-blocking every 50ms.
  - TUI (`interactive/commands.rs` + `interactive/agent.rs`): spawns a
    thread that blocks on the receiver and injects an `OAuthCallbackReceived`
    event when the browser redirect arrives, which auto-submits the code.
- Add `PiMsg::OAuthCallbackReceived` variant to the interactive event enum.
- Populate `redirect_uri` on all OAuth start functions (OpenAI Codex,
  Anthropic, Google Gemini CLI, Google Antigravity, extensions, Copilot,
  GitLab).

Fixes #22.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
